Sandpoint Savories is the town's most popular bakery, and mornings here smell like the best argument for living in Sandpoint. Alma Avertin has run it for decades, and her pastries and cookies are fixtures of every festival and most breakfasts.

Alma herself carries more grief than her warm counter manner shows — she is the twin sister of the sheriff Chopper slew, and her daughter Arika left town for the adventuring life.
summary: The town's most beloved bakery — Alma Avertin's pastries are a civic institution on Main Street.
title: Sandpoint Savories

content: The Sandpoint Shipyard is one of the town's major employers and a legacy of the Valdemar family's original investment in the settlement. With the Valdemars largely departed, the shipyard operates independently, building and repairing vessels for the fishing fleet and occasional larger commissions.
summary: Where Sandpoint's boats get built and repaired — a working industrial facility tied historically to the Valdemar family legacy.
title: Sandpoint Shipyard

content:
The Sandpoint Theater is the town's most celebrated cultural institution and a point of genuine civic pride. Its proprietor, Cyrdak Drokkus, built it into something that can hold its own against the playhouses of Magnimar — a fact he takes great pleasure in, given that he was forced to flee Magnimar in 4700 AR under circumstances he enjoys hinting at without ever quite explaining.

Cyrdak is married to Jasper Korvaski, a retired paladin of Abadar and town planner whose involvement in the Mercantile League has shielded both men from the Scarnettis' persistent disapproval of their marriage.
summary: Sandpoint's crown jewel of culture — a proper playhouse that holds its own against Magnimar's finest, run by a man with a dramatic past and a devoted husband.
title: Sandpoint Theater

content: Sandpoint Town Hall hosts the town's public meetings and houses offices for its councilors, with a secure vault belowground that doubles as the town's bank. The councilors all have businesses and duties of their own, so in practice the building belongs to its clerk: Shayliss Vinder, who knows where every record, key, and favor in Sandpoint's government is kept.
summary: The two-story seat of Sandpoint's government on Main Street — meeting hall above, the town's vault below, and Shayliss Vinder keeping the whole thing running.
title: Sandpoint Town Hall
Sarenrae
summary: The Dawnflower — goddess of healing, redemption, and the sun, whose faith of compassion and temperance is one of the most widely practiced at the Sandpoint Cathedral.
content:
Known to her faithful as the Dawnflower, the Healing Flame, and the Everlight, Sarenrae is a goddess who teaches temperance and patience in all things. Compassion and peace are her greatest virtues, and if enemies of the faith can be redeemed, they should be — though she is not without wrath for those who refuse redemption and continue to cause harm.

Sarenrae is one of the deities whose faith is welcomed at the multi-faith Sandpoint Cathedral. Her worship is widespread throughout the Inner Sea region. Her enemies include the undead goddess Urgathoa, whose portfolio of excess and disease runs counter to everything Sarenrae values.

She is one third of the divine relationship known as the Prismatic Ray, alongside Desna and Shelyn.

Savah Bevaniky runs a well-stocked armory that has seen a notable uptick in business as Sandpoint's reputation as an adventuring hub has grown. She's glad for the coin. She is somewhat less glad about whatever keeps drawing all those adventurers here in the first place.
summary: Sandpoint's best source for weapons and armor — run by a merchant who has noticed, with mixed feelings, how good business has been since the adventurers started arriving.
title: Savah's Armory
Savah Bevaniky
summary: Sandpoint's arms dealer — practical, professional, and quietly worried about why her town needs so many adventurers.
content: Savah runs a clean operation and keeps good stock. She's noticed that every time someone cleans her out of heavy crossbows, something terrible is about to happen to Sandpoint. She is currently well-stocked.

The Scarnetti Consortium is a collection of industrial companies and concerns centered on Sandpoint's lumber and milling industries, all owned and directed by Titus Scarnetti, the head of the Scarnetti family. The Consortium controls the lumber supply that competing interests — especially the now-weakened Valdemars — depend on, and Titus leverages this dependency openly and without apology.

The Consortium has a deeply negative reputation with much of Sandpoint's population, particularly the Varisian community. Evidence of collusion with local criminal elements nearly surfaced publicly when Scarnetti Manor was damaged in a giant raid; Titus managed to suppress most of it, but enough leaked to cause lasting damage to the family's standing. Since then, the Consortium has been rebuilt through secret dealings with Korvosan interests.

The Consortium is distinct from the broader Sandpoint Mercantile League, which it participates in but frequently works against. Its influence rating in town is sharply negative.
summary: An aggressive industrial conglomerate of mills and lumber concerns owned and controlled by Titus Scarnetti — widely regarded as Sandpoint's most unpleasant power broker.
title: The Scarnetti Consortium

content: Scarnetti Manor was damaged during a giant raid and spent several years in disrepair while the family's reputation suffered. Titus Scarnetti has since rebuilt both, and rumors abound about the sudden reversal of fortunes.
summary: The Scarnetti family estate — recently rebuilt after giant-raid damage, and paid for with money from a source Titus would rather not discuss.
title: Scarnetti Manor

content: Scarnetti Mill is one of the family's foundational commercial assets, managed by the perpetually anxious Courrin Whesterwill under conditions that might charitably be described as exploitative.
summary: The Scarnetti family's grain mill — one of their primary commercial operations, run by a man paid barely enough to stay and threatened enough not to leave.
title: Scarnetti Mill
Shayliss Vinder
summary: The Vinders' surviving, yet prodigal, daughter
content: Shayliss has become someone her parents don't quite recognize. She's found faith in Calistria seemingly out of nowhere. Whatever she's doing when she slips out of the secret door by the Turandarok River, it isn't what her mother imagines.

title: Shelyn
summary: The Eternal Rose — goddess of beauty, art, love, and music, whose faith of creative joy and compassion welcomes all who seek beauty in the world.
content:
Shelyn is the goddess of beauty, art, love, and music. She was born the daughter of the spirit-wolf Thron and shares her father with her half-brother Dou-Bral — who, after a long absence and mysterious corruption, returned as the god of pain and shadow, Zon-Kuthon. Shelyn still grieves this transformation and holds love for what her brother once was, even as she opposes what he has become.

Shelyn's faith celebrates creativity and joy in all its forms. She is one third of the divine relationship known as the Prismatic Ray alongside Desna and Sarenrae. Her worshippers are found wherever art is made, and her faith is welcomed at the Sandpoint Cathedral. She is closest among the gods to Brigh, goddess of invention, despite their apparent differences.

title: Solsta Vinder
summary: Ven Vinder's wife — keeping the general store running, keeping the family together, and quietly terrified that she's losing both her husband and her daughter.
content: Solsta manages the store, manages her husband's deterioration, and is starting to manage her fear about where Shayliss keeps disappearing to.

The Curious Goblin is a well-stocked bookshop whose prices reflect its proprietor's priorities — Chask Haladan values the books for their own sake, not for what they'll fetch. His adventuring youth gave him enough of a nest egg to run the shop on his own terms, which means most locals can't afford to shop there regularly. He also offers arcane spellcasting for those who ask.
summary: Sandpoint's bookshop — crammed with tomes, overpriced for the locals, and run by an old scholar who cares more about the books than the business.
title: The Curious Goblin
The Deverin Family
summary: The most respected of Sandpoint's founding families — down-to-earth farmers and brewers whose current leader serves as the town's popular mayor.
content:
The Deverin family is one of the most down-to-earth aristocratic families in Magnimar. Along with their modest town manor in the Naos district of Magnimar, they also own one of Magnimar's most successful breweries. A branch of the family sailed north in 4666 AR to found Sandpoint, and continues to live there today.

The current leader of the Sandpoint branch also serves as the town's mayor. Gaven Deverin, a cousin of the mayor, is the last surviving owner of Two Knight Brewing in Sandpoint.

The Feathered Serpent is the kind of shop that rewards browsing. Vorvashali Voon fills it with curiosities and magical items that seem almost randomly assembled but have a way of being exactly relevant.
summary: Sandpoint's curio shop — stocked with oddities, scrolls, and magical wares by a gregarious proprietor who always seems to know what you need before you do.
title: The Feathered Serpent

content: The Hagfish is a rowdy waterfront tavern popular with sailors, fishers, and people who enjoy a challenge. The house challenge involves drinking from a tank of slime produced by the bar's resident hagfish, Norah. Those who manage it have their names written on the ceiling. The proprietor, Jargie Quinn, has a different story every night about how he lost his leg, and the yarning is half the draw.
summary: A dockside dive bar — home to tall tales, a legendary challenge involving a tank of hagfish slime, and the best excuse to stay drunk on the waterfront.
title: The Hagfish
The Kaijitsu Family
summary: Sandpoint's most exciting founding family — glassmakers from far-off Minkai — whose last daughter left to become an empress, closing the chapter on their presence in town.
content:
The Kaijitsu family traveled across the Crown of the World from Minkai generations ago, eventually settling in Magnimar before becoming one of Sandpoint's founding families as glassmakers and jewelers. The family's profile in Magnimar declined after the death of the family head in a storm in 4687 AR.

Lonjiku Kaijitsu, the family's Sandpoint patriarch, was known for his sharp temper and difficult relationships with his children. His daughter Ameiko became an adventurer and innkeeper, defying all expectations — and eventually ascended to become Empress of Minkai. Lonjiku met a grisly end in the Sandpoint Glassworks during the 4707 AR goblin raids. With Ameiko's departure, the Kaijitsu family has effectively ended its presence in Sandpoint.

Every local has a story about the Pit, and most of them end badly. It is an open shaft in the heart of Devil's Platter, the rocky escarpment a couple of miles southeast of Sandpoint — perpetually fogged below its rim, ringed by jagged stone, and home (the stories insist) to the Sandpoint Devil itself.

Adventurers used to try their luck in its caves semi-regularly. Few came back, and these days most seek their fortunes elsewhere; the most recent company to descend was wiped out, leaving a single survivor. Those who want more than tavern tales are usually pointed at the sage Brodert Quink and his shelves of old books.
summary: A fog-choked shaft sunk into Devil's Platter southeast of town — a byword for danger, and the grave of more than one adventuring company.
title: The Pit

content: The Pixie's Kitten is Sandpoint's open secret — a brothel operated with a discretion that most of the town appreciates and none of the Scarnettis will acknowledge. It offers lodging as well as its primary services, and its proprietor, Kaye Tresarani, is a prominent enough figure in town that her establishment operates without serious harassment.
summary: Sandpoint's brothel — a surprisingly tasteful establishment whose proprietor keeps it safer and quieter than its reputation suggests.
title: The Pixie's Kitten
The Red Bishop
summary: A nameless mothman priest of Pazuzu — the patient architect of two decades of Sandpoint's sorrows.
content:
The Red Bishop shares his true name with no one; it cannot be spoken aloud, only conveyed in an unsettling telepathic sending. He came to the Lost Coast in 4700 AR, drawn by his patron's influence over the region, and found two prizes: an ancient shrine to Pazuzu hidden in the rock of Chopper's Isle, and something far older in the depths of the Pit — a thin place between the Material Plane and Pazuzu's Abyssal realm of High M'vania, held shut by an ancient ward he cannot directly undo.

Preferring slow corruption to swift ruin, he has spent more than twenty years arranging Sandpoint's tragedies: it was the Red Bishop who steered Jervis Stoot to the statuette that made him Chopper, and the Red Bishop whose nightmare-whispers turned the town's heart against Nualia. Every doom Sandpoint has suffered has fed his design — and the seven worst are still to come.

The Rusty Dragon is the beating heart of Sandpoint's social life: the best food in town, a reputation for welcoming adventurers, and a history that includes its former proprietor Ameiko Kaijitsu ascending to become Empress of Minkai. The inn is now managed by Bethana Corwin, a halfling who handles the quieter side of the business, and Daviren Hosk, who handles security and heavy labor while nursing a profound and personal hatred of goblins.
summary: Sandpoint's most popular inn and tavern — warm, welcoming, and still carrying the legend of its famous former owner.
title: The Rusty Dragon
The Scarnetti Family
summary: One of Sandpoint's founding families — conservative, powerful, and deeply unpopular with many locals — who control the town's lumber industry and have a history of shady dealings.
content:
The Scarnetti family controls Sandpoint's mills and lumber industry, making them indispensable to the town's economy despite being widely disliked. Many of Sandpoint's elderly Varisian locals still have not forgotten their part in the tragedy surrounding the town's founding.

The family is headed by Titus Scarnetti and maintains the most conservative, old-school Chelaxian values of any family in town. Recent years have been turbulent: rumors of collusion with local criminals after the Scarnetti Manor was damaged in a giant raid, and Titus allegedly secretly working with Korvosan interests to rebuild the family's fortunes has earned the suspicions of many locals.

title: The Valdemar Family
summary: Once Sandpoint's most prosperous founding family — shipbuilders and carpenters — the Valdemars have fallen on hard times and largely withdrawn from Sandpoint's civic life.
content:
The Valdemar family was once one of the most prosperous of Sandpoint's founding families, generating significant revenue through fishing, shipping, and logging. Found primarily in Magnimar and Sandpoint, the family's fortunes have declined sharply due to competition from the Scarnettis and a series of tragedies.

Ethram Valdemar, the family's Sandpoint patriarch, recently died. The Magnimar branch of the family has shown no interest in maintaining an active presence in Sandpoint, leaving their traditional council seat vacant and their businesses understaffed.

The Way North sells local and regional maps, novelty treasure maps, and the supplies to make your own, all under the proprietorship of Veznutt Parooh — a gnome cartographer and wizard of modest but practical talent who also sells arcane spellcasting services. Veznutt is forever hungry for new maps of anywhere, and pays for good ones.
summary: A cozy map shop on Cliff Street — charts, cartography supplies, and a gnome who will talk routes as long as you let him.
title: The Way North

content: The White Deer offers massive rooms for large parties, as well as the most luxurious accomodations. Its owner, Garridan Viskalai, keeps the place immaculate in an attempt to lure The Next Big Adventuring Party to get some of the spotlight that the Rusty Dragon enjoys. His gruff demeanor undercuts the effort somewhat, but the quality of the rooms speaks for itself. Garridan is the brother of Sheriff Belor Hemlock — a fact neither of them discusses without visible discomfort.
summary: The newest building in town, capitalizing on the influx of flush pursed adventurers coming through town.
title: The White Deer

content:
The Tickwood is a relatively small forested area in the Sandpoint Hinterlands, located on the northern banks of the Turandarok River. It is less than two miles in length, separated from the coastal Shank's Wood to its north by the Lost Coast Road. To its west lie the Ravenroost hills, while Leb's Tors rise to the east.

The Tickwood is a favorite spot for local hunters, as it is thought to be free of goblin tribes, and is home to many deer, wild boars, and the occasional firepelt cougar. Stories in Sandpoint greatly exaggerate the size of the giant ticks that also dwell here, keeping many cautious citizens off the forest paths.

At the heart of the wood stands Akoni's Grove, the court of the dryad Akoni, who presides over the Tickwood's fey inhabitants. A troop of redcaps has recently moved into the area and though they will not establish permanent roots without the dryad's permission, they seem confident they can obtain it one way or another.
summary: A small forest just outside Sandpoint, popular with local hunters — though its fey lord Akoni and a recent redcap incursion make it less safe than it appears.
title: The Tickwood

content:
Titus is the last significant patriarch of the Scarnetti line, operating Sandpoint's lumber industry and nursing grievances against the mayor, the Mercantile League, and the town's general failure to accord him the deference he considers his birthright.

Scarnetti is quite conservative and traditional in his thinking, which often puts him at odds with the other people in town. This disconnect has cost him the race for mayor every time he has run against the more likeable Kendra Deverin, and there is no love lost between the two.

Lately, there have been rumors that Titus has links to certain nefarious individuals in the Sandpoint area—including members of the Sczarni criminal gangs—which have greatly reduced his standing in town.3 The exits of fellow local nobles Ameiko Kaijitsu and the Valdemars have further eroded his influence, leading him to form the Scarnetti Consortium in an attempt to consolidate his power.
summary: Head of the Scarnetti family — a man who has rebuilt his fortune through means he'd prefer to keep quiet, and who hasn't forgiven Sandpoint for not being afraid of him.
title: Titus Scarnetti

content: Turandarok Academy serves as both school and orphanage, funded partly by the town and partly by the personal fortune of its headmaster, the retired adventurer Ilsoari Gandethus. Ilsoari teaches, manages the orphans, and maintains a locked basement full of exotic weapons, strange maps, and monster trophies that he's happy to show adults who ask. The children spin elaborate theories about what's down there. Most of them are wrong. One of them is startlingly close.
summary: Sandpoint's school and orphanage — and, in its locked basement, the private museum of a retired adventurer who has seen things the curriculum doesn't cover.
title: Turandarok Academy

content: Two Knight Brewing produces the ales and lagers that supply most of Sandpoint's taverns and serves them directly in its own taproom. The Alvertin family runs the brewery, and while the beer is reliable and well-regarded, the family has had a difficult few years.
summary: Sandpoint's brewery and taproom — good beer, honest prices, and the Alvertin family keeping things running despite some quiet personal grief.
title: Two Knight Brewing

content: The Valdemar Fish Market sells the local fleet's catch within sight of the water it came from. Turch Sterglus runs it for the Valdemar family with a fisherman's bluntness and a fair scale, and the market sets the rhythm of the waterfront's mornings.
summary: The open-air fish market on Water Street, where the morning catch lands almost straight off the boats.
title: Valdemar Fish Market

content: The Valdemars were one of Sandpoint's four founding families, their fortune built on shipbuilding and carpentry. The family has largely moved on from Sandpoint, and their manor reflects this departure — maintained but no longer a center of power. Their old seat on the town council sits vacant.
summary: The former seat of the Valdemar family — largely vacated now, after Sandpoint's fourth founding family drifted away.
title: Valdemar Manor
Vanda
summary: A former Stonehome acolyte who has not yet fully surrendered to the cult of Xar-Azmak — still wearing her unrusted plate, still carrying a thread of remorse.
content:
Vanda was one of the acolytes of Stonehome, the garrison-church of Gorum in Iron Harbor, who fell under the influence of Meitremar's cult of Xar-Azmak when the Iron Harbor was infiltrated. Unlike the others who fully converted — trading their unrusted steel for the rusted armor and sickles that mark devotion to the dead demon lord — Vanda never made that final surrender.

In the wake of the crisis, Vanda stepped up to lead Stonehome, taking on the responsibilities of a temple left without clear direction after the corruption was purged. She has since thrown herself into rebuilding both the church and the community's trust in it.

She is currently in a situationship with Ashka B'gosh.

Varisia has no central government. Its independent city-states — Magnimar, Korvosa, and the pirate haven of Riddleport — each hold sway over a small collection of towns and villages. The wilds beyond, including most of the Storval Plateau, are claimed by the Shoanti people. The neutral city of Kaer Maga is often discounted from political calculations altogether, and its inhabitants prefer it that way.

The human population is split between Varisians/Shoanti — tribes descended from Thassilon's enslaved workers who developed nomadic cultures of their own — and Chelaxian-descended settlers from the south. 

Ancient Thassilonian ruins are scattered throughout Varisia, relics of an empire destroyed by the cataclysm known as Earthfall ten thousand years ago. These ruins have taken on new significance with the recent return of New Thassilon and The Runelords.
summary: A vast frontier nation on the western coast of Avistan, Varisia is home to ancient Thassilonian ruins, nomadic Varisian and Shoanti peoples, and three major city-states that compete for dominance.
title: Varisia
Ven Vinder
summary: Owner of Sandpoint's general store — a father who lost a daughter to a serial killer and has been losing himself to grief ever since.
content: Ven used to be one of those men who measured their worth in their family. Now he measures it in how many hours he can spend in his cellar drinking before anyone comes looking for him.

Rynshinn Povalli's shop caters to every style Sandpoint wears, and a few it doesn't know it wants yet. Alongside everyday and formal clothing, she fits non-metallic light armor for those whose work runs rougher than most.
summary: Sandpoint's tailor on Prickleback Lane — clothing of all styles, from festival finery to traveling leathers.
title: Vernah's Fine Clothing
Vlorus
summary: The Abyssal realm once ruled by the deceased demon lord Xar-Azmak — a valley of rusted junk and decaying castles, now unclaimed, whose barrier to the Plane of Metal is dangerously thin.
content: Vlorus is the Abyssal realm once ruled by the deceased demon lord Xar-Azmak before his death by Dispater's armies. It is now unclaimed by any power. The valley realm is full of rusted junk and buildings, some as large as the castles that circle the tops of the valley in which the realm is located.

title: Vorvashali Voon
summary: The gregarious proprietor of the Feathered Serpent — a collector and dealer in magical oddities who is currently very interested in clockwork birds.
content: Vorvashali is enthusiastic about almost everything and especially enthusiastic about magical curiosities. Nine out of ten adventurers will tell you one of Vorvashali's items saved their hid, and the tenth didn't make it out of the dungeon.
Bilivar Wheen builds and repairs wagons and wheels, and has begun diversifying into a courier service for parcels and messages around town and along the Lost Coast Road. Honest work, honestly priced.
summary: A wheelwright's shop on River Street, lately branching out into courier work.
title: Wheen's Wagons
The Whistlefangs
summary: A sadistic goblin clan clinging to the Pit's uppermost caves — named for the whistling cries they pipe through holes drilled in their own teeth.
content:
Goblin clans have squatted in the Pit's upper reaches for centuries, and the Whistlefangs are the latest — and nearly the last of their line. Two years ago they held everything above the fog; a soured alliance with two bickering caligni bands and the disastrous arrival of the Devil's Disciples has since reduced them to a fraction of their number, squeezed into the uppermost level against infestations of gremlins.

They were ready to abandon the Pit entirely when a miracle arrived: an undead figure in fiery armor calling itself Ripnugget. The clan's signature is the whistling speech they pipe through drilled teeth — not a true language, but a code of signals they use to coordinate ambushes and defense throughout their warren.

At the height of the Ancient Thassilonian Empire, Emperor Xin crafted seven Alara'quin: magical weapons of extraordinary power, each bound to one of his seven Runelords. Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer was the symbol of office for Xanderghul, the sole and eternal Runelord of Pride.

Xanderghul's domain was illusion magic, and the Hammer reflects this — it is not merely a weapon but a psychic instrument, attuned to pride and the manipulation of emotion. Its most notable property is its reaction to emotional effects: when the wielder would be adversely affected by an emotion, the Hammer attempts to counteract it with a surge of intense pride.

The weapon is currently carried by Jade Inferno, who recovered it and has integrated it into both their spellcasting and their ongoing investigation into New Thassilon's ambitions. Given that Xanderghul himself may have survived Earthfall — no body was ever confirmed — the Hammer's provenance is a subject Jade keeps in mind.

As an Alara'quin, it is an artifact-class item. It cannot be destroyed by conventional means.
summary: One of the seven Alara'quin forged by Emperor Xin — the symbol of office of Xanderghul, Runelord of Pride, now carried by Jade Inferno.
title: Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer
